title: What is a Computer?
content: A computer is like a smart helper that can do many tasks. Imagine it as a magical box that can store information, show pictures, play music, and help you learn new things. Computers come in different sizes - some are big like desks, some are small like phones, and some are tiny like watches. The main parts of a computer are the screen (where you see things), the keyboard (where you type words), and the mouse (a small device you move to point at things on the screen). Computers need electricity to work, just like a light bulb. They can remember things even when turned off, and they can connect to the internet to talk to other computers around the world. Learning to use a computer is like learning to read and write - it opens up a whole new world of possibilities!

homework.question: Think of three things you could use a computer for in your daily life. Write them down and tell someone why each one would be helpful.

questions.question: What is a computer?
options: A magical box that helps with tasks

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a part of a computer?
What do computers need to work?

title: Computer Hardware - The Physical Parts
content: Hardware is the name for all the physical parts of a computer that you can touch. The most important part is the computer tower or box - this is where the 'brain' lives. Inside this box is the CPU (Central Processing Unit), which is like the computer's brain that does all the thinking. There's also memory (RAM) which is like the computer's short-term memory for remembering what it's working on right now. The hard drive or SSD is like the computer's long-term memory where it stores files, photos, and programs even when turned off. The screen (monitor) shows you what the computer is doing. The keyboard lets you type letters and numbers. The mouse helps you point and click on things. Some computers have speakers for sound, microphones for talking, and cameras for video. All these parts work together to make the computer useful. When buying a computer, you want to make sure all these parts are good quality and work well together.

homework.question: Look at a computer (or ask someone to show you one). Try to identify the screen, keyboard, mouse, and computer box. Draw a simple picture showing where each part is.

questions.question: What is hardware?
options: The physical parts you can touch

questions.question: What does CPU stand for?
Which part shows you what the computer is doing?

title: Computer Software - The Programs
content: Software is the name for all the programs and instructions that tell the computer what to do. Without software, hardware is just a bunch of useless metal and plastic. The most important software is the operating system - this is like the computer's manager that controls everything. Windows, macOS, and Linux are examples of operating systems. They provide the basic tools like showing the desktop, opening windows, and managing files. Then there are application programs that do specific jobs. Word processors like Microsoft Word help you write letters and documents. Web browsers like Google Chrome let you visit websites. Email programs help you send and receive messages. Games are software too. Some software comes free with the computer, and some you have to buy. Software gets updated regularly to fix problems and add new features. It's important to keep your software updated for security and to get the best performance.

homework.question: Make a list of 5 different types of software you think people use. For each one, write what it helps people do.

questions.question: What is software?
options: Programs that tell the computer what to do

questions.question: What is an operating system?
Which of these is an operating system?

title: Turning On and Off a Computer
content: Learning to properly start and stop a computer is very important. To turn on a computer, look for the power button. On a desktop computer, it's usually on the front of the tower box. On a laptop, it's often above the keyboard. Press this button once and wait for the computer to start up. You'll see lights come on and the screen will show the operating system loading. This might take a minute or two. Never turn off the power while the computer is working - this can cause problems. To turn off properly, click the Start button (usually a Windows logo), then click 'Shut down' or 'Power off'. Wait for the computer to completely shut down before unplugging it. If the computer freezes and won't respond, you can hold the power button for 5-10 seconds to force it off, but this should only be done in emergencies. Proper shutdown protects your work and keeps the computer healthy.

homework.question: Practice turning on and off a computer safely. Ask someone to watch you do it correctly.

questions.question: Where is the power button usually located on a desktop computer?
options: On the front of the tower

questions.question: What should you do if a computer freezes?
options: Hold the power button for 5-10 seconds

questions.question: Why is it important to shut down properly?
options: To protect your work and computer health
To make it start faster next time

title: Using the Mouse
content: The mouse is your main tool for pointing and clicking on things on the computer screen. Hold the mouse gently in your hand with your thumb on one side and fingers on the other. Move the mouse on a flat surface and watch the pointer (usually an arrow) move on the screen. The left button is the most important - you use it to select things and open programs. Click means press and release the left button quickly. Double-click means click twice quickly. Right-click means press the right button. The scroll wheel in the middle lets you scroll up and down on pages. Practice moving the mouse smoothly and clicking accurately. If you have trouble, try using a mouse pad for better control. Some computers have touchpads on laptops - these work like mice but you touch them with your finger instead of moving a physical mouse. Learning to use the mouse takes practice, but soon it will feel natural.

homework.question: Practice moving the mouse pointer around the screen. Try clicking on different icons and see what happens. Practice double-clicking too.

questions.question: What is the most important mouse button?
What does double-click mean?
What does the scroll wheel do?
options: Lets you scroll up and down

title: Using the Keyboard
content: The keyboard is how you type letters, numbers, and symbols into the computer. Each key has a letter or symbol on it. Press keys one at a time to type. The spacebar (the long bar at the bottom) adds spaces between words. The Enter key starts a new line or confirms actions. The Shift key makes capital letters and symbols. The Backspace key deletes letters behind the cursor. The Delete key deletes letters in front. Arrow keys move the cursor around. Practice typing your name, then simple sentences. Don't worry about speed at first - accuracy is more important. Look at the keys while typing until you get comfortable. There are different keyboard layouts, but QWERTY is the most common. Some keys have special functions like Ctrl, Alt, and Windows key. Learning to type takes time, but it's a very useful skill. There are even games and programs that can help you learn typing faster.

homework.question: Type your full name using the keyboard. Then type a simple sentence like 'I am learning about computers.'

questions.question: Which key adds spaces between words?
Which key makes capital letters?
What does the Backspace key do?

title: Understanding Files and Folders
content: Files are like digital papers that store your information. Photos, documents, music, and programs are all stored as files. Folders are like drawers that help you organize your files. You can put files into folders, and folders into other folders. This is called a folder structure. On Windows computers, you can see your files and folders in File Explorer. It shows a tree-like structure with folders inside folders. The Desktop is a special folder where you can put shortcuts to your favorite files and programs. Documents folder is for your word processing files. Pictures folder is for photos. Downloads folder is where files go when you download them from the internet. It's important to keep your files organized so you can find them easily. You can create new folders by right-clicking and choosing 'New Folder'. You can move files by dragging them. Deleting files moves them to the Recycle Bin where you can get them back if you change your mind.

homework.question: Open File Explorer and look at the different folders. Create a new folder and name it 'My Computer Practice'. Put a note or drawing inside it.

questions.question: What is a file?
options: A digital paper that stores information

questions.question: What are folders used for?
Where do downloaded files usually go?

title: The Desktop and Icons
content: The desktop is the main screen you see when you start your computer. It's like a digital desk where you can put shortcuts to your favorite programs and files. Icons are the little pictures that represent programs, files, and folders. Double-clicking an icon opens that program or file. You can move icons around by dragging them. Right-clicking an icon shows a menu with options. The taskbar at the bottom shows open programs and the Start button. The Start button (Windows logo) opens a menu where you can find all your programs. You can add shortcuts to the desktop by right-clicking a program in the Start menu and choosing 'Pin to desktop'. The desktop should be kept tidy - don't put too many icons there or it gets confusing. You can create folders on the desktop to organize your shortcuts. The Recycle Bin icon holds deleted files. Double-click it to see what's inside and restore files if needed.

homework.question: Look at your desktop. Count how many icons are there. Try double-clicking one to see what it does. Try right-clicking one to see the menu.

questions.question: What is the desktop?
options: The main screen you see when starting

questions.question: How do you open a program from an icon?
What does the Start button do?

title: What is the Internet?
content: The internet is like a huge network that connects computers all around the world. It's like an invisible web that lets computers talk to each other and share information. Websites are like digital stores or libraries where you can find information, watch videos, listen to music, and buy things. The internet uses special addresses called URLs to find websites. You need a web browser (like Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge) to visit websites. The internet works through wires, cables under the ground, and wireless signals. Some people get internet through phone lines, cable TV wires, or satellite dishes. The internet is always changing and growing. New websites are created every day. You can use the internet to learn, communicate, shop, and have fun. But it's important to be careful online and not share personal information with strangers. The internet is a tool that can help you in many ways if used safely.

homework.question: Think of three websites you know about or would like to visit. Write down what you think you would find on each one.

questions.question: What is the internet?
What do you need to visit websites?
What is a URL?

title: Using a Web Browser
content: A web browser is a program that lets you visit websites and view web pages. Popular browsers include Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, and Safari. To use a browser, type a website address (URL) in the address bar at the top and press Enter. You can also search for websites using a search engine like Google. The browser shows web pages with text, pictures, and videos. You can click links (underlined text or buttons) to go to other pages. The back button takes you to the previous page. The forward button goes forward. The refresh button reloads the page. You can open multiple tabs to view different websites at once. Bookmarks let you save favorite websites for quick access later. Some browsers have extensions that add extra features. It's important to keep your browser updated for security. You should also clear your browsing history sometimes to keep things tidy and protect your privacy.

homework.question: Open a web browser. Type in a website address you know (like google.com) and visit it. Try clicking on a link to go to another page.

questions.question: What is a web browser used for?
Where do you type website addresses?
What does the back button do?
options: Takes you to the previous page

title: Searching the Internet
content: Search engines help you find information on the internet. Google is the most popular search engine. To search, go to google.com and type your question or keywords in the search box, then press Enter or click the search button. The search engine will show you web pages that match what you typed. Look at the results and click on ones that seem helpful. You can use quotes around words to search for exact phrases. Adding words like 'near me' or 'images' changes the type of results. Search engines can find websites, images, videos, maps, and more. Not all information online is true, so be careful and check multiple sources. You can use advanced search features to find more specific results. Search engines remember your searches, so use private browsing if you want privacy. Learning to search effectively is a very useful skill that helps you learn new things and solve problems.

homework.question: Go to Google and search for something you're interested in learning about. Click on one of the results and read a little bit.

questions.question: What is the most popular search engine?
How do you search on Google?
options: Type in the search box and press Enter

questions.question: Why should you check multiple sources?
options: Because not all information online is true

title: Email Basics
content: Email is like digital mail that you can send instantly to anyone with an internet connection. You need an email address to send and receive emails. Popular email services include Gmail (from Google), Outlook (from Microsoft), and Yahoo Mail. To send an email, click 'Compose' or 'New Email', type the person's email address in the 'To' field, add a subject, write your message, and click 'Send'. Emails can include attachments like photos or documents. You can organize emails into folders. The inbox shows new emails. It's important to check your email regularly. Email addresses look like this: yourname@example.com. The part before the @ is your username, and after is the service. Never share your password with anyone. Use strong passwords and enable two-factor authentication for security. Email is great for staying in touch with family and friends, and for business communication.

homework.question: If you have an email account, practice sending a simple email to yourself. If not, ask someone to show you how email works.

questions.question: What is email?
What do you need to use email?
What should you NEVER share?

title: Online Safety
content: Staying safe online is very important. Never share personal information like your full name, address, phone number, or school with strangers online. Don't meet people you only know from the internet in person without telling a trusted adult. Use strong passwords and don't share them. Be careful what you post online - once something is posted, it can be hard to remove. Think before you click links or download files - they might contain viruses. Use antivirus software and keep it updated. Don't respond to messages from people you don't know. Use privacy settings on social media. Tell a trusted adult if something online makes you uncomfortable. The internet is fun and useful, but safety comes first. Remember that not everyone online is who they say they are. Use common sense and trust your instincts. If something feels wrong, it probably is. There are many resources online and in your community to help you stay safe.

homework.question: Write down three rules for staying safe online. Share them with someone and explain why each rule is important.

questions.question: Should you share your address with strangers online?
What should you do if something online makes you uncomfortable?
Why should you use strong passwords?

title: Social Media Basics
content: Social media are websites where people share updates, photos, and connect with friends. Popular ones include Facebook, Instagram, Twitter (now X), TikTok, and Snapchat. You can create a profile with your information and photos. You can post updates about what you're doing, share photos and videos, and comment on other people's posts. You can 'like' or 'follow' people to see their updates. Social media helps you stay connected with family and friends, learn about news, and discover new interests. But remember the safety rules - don't share too much personal information, be careful what you post, and think before you share. Privacy settings let you control who sees your posts. You can block people who bother you. Social media can be fun, but don't spend too much time on it. Balance online time with real-life activities. Always be kind and respectful to others online.

homework.question: If you use social media, check your privacy settings. If not, ask someone to show you a social media site and explain what people do there.

questions.question: What is social media used for?
options: Sharing updates and connecting with people

questions.question: What should you be careful about on social media?
What can you do if someone bothers you online?

title: Word Processing - Writing Documents
content: Word processing programs help you create, edit, and format written documents. Microsoft Word is the most popular, but there are free alternatives like Google Docs and LibreOffice Writer. To start, open the program and begin typing. You can change fonts (styles of writing), sizes, and colors. Bold, italic, and underline make text stand out. You can add pictures, tables, and lists. Spell check helps find mistakes. Save your work often by clicking File > Save. You can print documents or share them electronically. Word processors are used for letters, reports, resumes, and many other documents. Learning basic formatting makes your documents look professional. You can undo mistakes with Ctrl+Z. Copy and paste lets you reuse text. Headers and footers add information to every page. Word processing is an essential skill for school, work, and personal use.

homework.question: Open a word processor and type a short letter or story. Try changing the font size and making some words bold.

questions.question: What is word processing used for?
Which key combination undoes mistakes?
What does bold text do?

title: Spreadsheets - Working with Numbers
content: Spreadsheets are programs for organizing numbers and data in rows and columns. Microsoft Excel is popular, but Google Sheets is free. Each box is called a cell. You can type numbers, text, or formulas. Formulas start with = and can add, subtract, multiply, and divide. For example, =A1+B1 adds the numbers in cells A1 and B1. You can create charts and graphs from your data. Spreadsheets are used for budgets, grades, inventories, and calculations. You can sort data alphabetically or numerically. Filters help find specific information. Conditional formatting changes cell colors based on values. Spreadsheets save time on repetitive calculations. Learning basic formulas is very useful. You can copy formulas to multiple cells. Spreadsheets are powerful tools for organizing information and making decisions based on data.

homework.question: Open a spreadsheet program and create a simple list, like your favorite foods with prices. Try adding up the prices with a formula.

questions.question: What is a spreadsheet used for?
What do formulas in spreadsheets start with?
What can you create from spreadsheet data?

title: Online Shopping
content: Online shopping lets you buy things from stores on the internet without leaving home. Popular sites include Amazon, eBay, Walmart.com, and Target.com. To shop, search for items or browse categories. Read reviews from other buyers. Check prices and shipping costs. Add items to your cart, then checkout. You'll need to provide payment information and shipping address. Use credit cards or PayPal for safety. Look for secure sites (https:// and lock icon). Compare prices on different sites. Read return policies. Be careful of scams - don't buy from suspicious sites. Online shopping saves time and offers more choices, but always shop safely. Keep records of your purchases. If something is wrong with your order, contact customer service. Online shopping is convenient but requires careful attention to security and policies.

homework.question: Look at an online shopping site (with adult supervision). Find something inexpensive and see how the checkout process works.

questions.question: What is online shopping?
What should you check before buying online?
How can you tell if a shopping site is secure?
options: It has https:// and a lock icon

title: Online Banking
content: Online banking lets you manage your money from home using the internet. Most banks offer online services. You can check your balance, transfer money, pay bills, and view transactions. To use online banking, create an account on your bank's website. Use strong passwords and enable security features like two-factor authentication. Never share your banking information. Be careful of phishing emails that look like they're from your bank. Only log in on the official bank website. Online banking is convenient and saves time compared to going to the bank. You can set up automatic bill payments. Monitor your accounts regularly for suspicious activity. If you see something wrong, contact your bank immediately. Online banking is safe when you follow security best practices. Many banks offer mobile apps for banking on your phone too.

homework.question: If you have a bank account, ask a parent to show you the online banking website. If not, learn about what online banking can do.

questions.question: What can you do with online banking?
What should you NEVER do with banking information?
What is phishing?
options: Fake emails pretending to be from banks

title: Digital Photos and Images
content: Digital photos are pictures stored as computer files. You can take photos with cameras, phones, or scanners. Photo files have extensions like .jpg, .png, or .gif. You can view photos with programs like Windows Photos or online viewers. Edit photos with programs like Photoshop or free alternatives. You can crop, resize, adjust brightness, and add effects. Organize photos in folders by date or event. Back up important photos to avoid losing them. Share photos via email, social media, or cloud storage like Google Photos. Be careful about copyright - don't use photos that aren't yours without permission. Digital photos are easy to copy and share, but respect privacy. Learning basic photo editing is useful for school projects and personal use. You can create albums and slideshows. Digital photography has made capturing and sharing memories much easier than traditional film.

homework.question: Find some digital photos on your computer or phone. Try organizing them into folders by topic or date.

questions.question: What are digital photos?
What can you do with photo editing programs?
Why should you back up photos?

title: Video and Music Online
content: You can watch videos and listen to music online using websites and apps. YouTube has billions of videos on every topic. Netflix, Hulu, and Disney+ offer movies and TV shows. Spotify, Apple Music, and YouTube Music stream songs. Many services are free with ads or require subscriptions. Search for content by topic or artist. Create playlists of your favorite songs. Be careful about copyright - don't download illegal copies. Use official services to support creators. Videos and music can educate and entertain, but don't spend too much time. Some content isn't appropriate for all ages. Use parental controls if needed. Online video and music have made entertainment accessible everywhere. You can learn new skills by watching tutorials. Music helps with relaxation and mood. Balance screen time with other activities. Always respect content warnings and age restrictions.

homework.question: Watch a short educational video on YouTube about something you're learning in school. Or listen to a song you like on a music streaming service.

questions.question: What is YouTube used for?
Why should you use official music services?
options: To support creators and avoid legal issues

questions.question: What should you do with screen time?

title: Computer Maintenance
content: Keeping your computer healthy requires regular maintenance. Update your operating system and programs regularly for security and new features. Run antivirus scans weekly to check for viruses. Clean up temporary files and empty the recycle bin. Defragment your hard drive if you have a traditional one (SSDs don't need this). Keep your computer clean from dust - use compressed air carefully. Don't eat or drink near the computer. Shut down properly instead of just unplugging. Back up important files regularly to external drives or cloud storage. If something seems wrong, don't try to fix it yourself unless you're sure - ask for help. Regular maintenance prevents problems and keeps your computer running smoothly. Update passwords periodically. Clean your keyboard and mouse. Monitor your computer's performance and free up space when needed.

homework.question: Check if your computer has any updates available. If so, install them (with adult help if needed).

questions.question: Why should you update your computer regularly?
What should you do with temporary files?
What is the best way to back up files?
options: Copy to external drives or cloud

title: Troubleshooting Common Problems
content: Computers sometimes have problems, but many can be fixed easily. If the computer won't start, check if it's plugged in and the power button works. If programs freeze, try waiting or force quit using Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc). If the internet doesn't work, restart your router or check connections. For slow performance, close unused programs and restart. If you get error messages, search for the exact message online. Update drivers if hardware doesn't work. Run antivirus if you suspect malware. Clear browser cache if websites don't load properly. Restarting often fixes temporary problems. If problems persist, get help from someone knowledgeable. Don't panic - most computer problems have solutions. Keep your computer cool and don't overload it. Regular maintenance prevents many issues. Learning basic troubleshooting saves time and frustration.

homework.question: Think of a time when something didn't work on a computer or device. What did you do to fix it? Or what would you try?

questions.question: What should you do first if a program freezes?
How can you find solutions to error messages?
What often fixes temporary computer problems?

title: Cloud Storage and Backup
content: Cloud storage lets you store files online instead of just on your computer. Services like Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, and iCloud provide free and paid storage. Upload files to the cloud and access them from any device with internet. This protects against losing files if your computer breaks. Share files with others by sending links. Collaborate on documents in real-time. Automatic backup keeps your files safe. Cloud storage is secure with encryption. Use strong passwords and two-factor authentication. Free accounts give limited storage, paid ones give more. Cloud storage is essential for modern computing. Back up photos, documents, and important files regularly. Test your backups by trying to restore a file. Cloud storage gives peace of mind knowing your files are safe even if something happens to your computer.

homework.question: If you have cloud storage, upload a file and then access it from another device. If not, learn about how cloud storage works.

questions.question: What is cloud storage?
Why is cloud storage useful?
options: Access from any device and backup protection

questions.question: What should you do with important files?
options: Back them up to cloud storage

title: Mobile Devices and Apps
content: Mobile devices like smartphones and tablets are computers you can carry. They run apps instead of programs. Apps are downloaded from app stores like Google Play or Apple App Store. Common apps include messaging, social media, games, maps, cameras, and productivity tools. Touch screens use taps, swipes, and pinches. Voice assistants like Siri or Google Assistant help with tasks. Mobile devices connect to cellular networks or Wi-Fi. They have cameras, GPS, and sensors. Battery life is important - charge regularly. Protect with passwords or fingerprints. Apps can be free or paid. Be careful downloading apps - check reviews and permissions. Mobile devices are powerful tools for communication, entertainment, and productivity. They complement desktop computers. Learning to use mobile devices is essential in today's world. Keep software updated for security.

homework.question: Look at the apps on your phone or tablet. Pick three different types of apps and explain what each one does.

questions.question: What are mobile apps?
Where do you download mobile apps?
Why should you update mobile apps?

title: Digital Citizenship
content: Digital citizenship means being a good person online. Be respectful and kind in your communications. Don't cyberbully or harass others. Respect copyright - don't steal digital content. Protect your privacy and others'. Think before posting - once online, content is hard to remove. Use technology to help others and make the world better. Be aware of digital divides - not everyone has equal access to technology. Learn about current events and issues online. Vote and participate in digital democracy. Use social media for positive change. Be a good role model for others online. Digital citizenship includes rights and responsibilities. Understand netiquette (internet etiquette). Stay informed about laws and policies affecting digital life. Being a good digital citizen makes the internet a better place for everyone.

homework.question: Write about one way you can be a good digital citizen. How would this help make the internet better for others?

questions.question: What is digital citizenship?
What should you do before posting online?
Why is respect important online?
options: Makes the internet better for everyone
